title: Work Bus

jokes.text: Andy presents 'Summer Softball Epic Fails' - a compilation video of their team's mistakes
jokes.setup: Andy proudly presents what should be a highlight reel
jokes.punchline: It's actually just their failures with him shouting 'Fail!'
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy character moment - his misguided enthusiasm and complete lack of self-awareness. The misdirection works well.

jokes.text: Oscar: 'A blooper reel? What is this, 2005? Do I look like Bob Saget?'
jokes.setup: Andy calls his video a blooper reel
jokes.punchline: Oscar's dated pop culture reference critique
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific snark from Oscar, though the Bob Saget reference feels slightly forced.

jokes.text: Pete and Clark doing mock introductions: 'Hi, I'm Pete! Puberty is such a drag, man.' 'And I'm Clark, I like to eat toilet paper.'
jokes.setup: The new guys are in Andy's fail video
jokes.punchline: Their deliberately awkward, random introductions
jokes.explanation: Absurdist humor that works because of its randomness and the actors' commitment.

jokes.text: Andy suddenly shifts to a 'solemn moment to remember Jerry in the warehouse, who passed away this year'
jokes.setup: Andy's been doing a silly fail video
jokes.punchline: Abrupt tonal shift to memorializing a dead coworker
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly uncomfortable tonal whiplash. The abrupt shift from silly to somber is perfectly executed cringe comedy.

jokes.text: Oscar: 'That was just normal video with you making vulgar noises.' Andy: 'Well, I worked with what I had, Oscar.'
jokes.setup: Oscar calls out Andy's lazy video editing
jokes.punchline: Andy's defensive rationalization
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic - Oscar's precision vs Andy's delusion. Andy's defensive excuse is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Jim's overthinking of protecting Pam: 'Like if we were in some biker bar and she mouthed off to some bikers... Wham! You've got to go through me first!'
jokes.setup: Jim wants to do something huge for Pam
jokes.punchline: His weirdly specific and unrealistic fantasy scenario
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Jim's romantic but impractical mindset. The specificity of the biker bar scenario is absurd and endearing.

jokes.text: Andy's hourglass monologue with baroque dependent clauses while refusing to pause the timer
jokes.setup: Andy gives Toby one minute to speak
jokes.punchline: He wastes the time with his own verbose explanation
jokes.explanation: Exceptional character comedy - Andy's self-sabotaging verbosity is perfectly executed. The irony of wasting time explaining time limits is brilliant.

jokes.text: Andy: 'A baby what? A human?'
jokes.setup: Toby says he's trying to adopt a baby
jokes.punchline: Andy's clarifying question about species
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and delivery. The apparent confusion about what kind of baby creates a great absurdist beat.

jokes.text: Andy's paranoid response: 'Fall right into your plagiarism entrapment scheme? I don't think so.'
jokes.setup: Toby offers to write his own reference letter
jokes.punchline: Andy sees it as an elaborate trap
jokes.explanation: Great paranoid Andy logic - turning a simple favor into a complex conspiracy. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: Andy's talking head: 'If she tells the truth about how evil and unfit to be a mother she is, then yeah, I'll sign that.'
jokes.setup: Andy agrees to read Toby's letter
jokes.punchline: His assessment of Toby as evil and unfit
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Andy's irrational hatred of Toby, though this dynamic has been done before.

jokes.text: Meredith: 'I already ditched my uterus, and I ain't losing any more good parts!'
jokes.setup: Discussion about EMF radiation health risks
jokes.punchline: Meredith's crude oversharing about her hysterectomy
jokes.explanation: Classic Meredith - inappropriate oversharing delivered with perfect confidence. Great character integration.

jokes.text: Stanley's enthusiastic 'A week off, that'd be great' in response to shutting down the office
jokes.setup: Dwight complains about having to shut down for a week
jokes.punchline: Stanley's immediate excitement about time off
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stanley reaction - his priorities are always clear. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: Jim microwaving one kernel of popcorn to 'get his wife a week off from work'
jokes.setup: Jim is creating fake EMF evidence
jokes.punchline: The absurdly minimal effort of one popped kernel
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual gag - the minimal effort creating maximum chaos is perfectly Jim. The economy and absurdity work beautifully together.

jokes.text: Dwight's superhero justification: 'Most of my childhood heroes got their powers that way'
jokes.setup: Dwight dismissing EMF concerns
jokes.punchline: His comic book logic about gaining superpowers
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight logic - turning potential danger into comic book fantasy. Excellent character integration.

jokes.text: Dwight: 'Breast pain.' 'No knobbies, no probbies.'
jokes.setup: Reading EMF side effects
jokes.punchline: Dwight's crude rhyming dismissal
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight wordplay - juvenile but delivered with complete confidence. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Dwight's pause at 'infertility' and uncertain 'Yeah, right'
jokes.setup: Dwight has been dismissing all EMF side effects
jokes.punchline: His hesitation at fertility concerns
jokes.explanation: Great subtle setup for Dwight's later fertility concerns. The pause and uncertainty break his confident pattern perfectly.

jokes.text: Jim discovering the popcorn has 'crowned' - some kernels have popped
jokes.setup: Jim planted one kernel as fake evidence
jokes.punchline: The EMF field actually worked, popping more corn
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - Jim's fake evidence becomes real evidence. The ironic reversal is beautifully executed.

jokes.text: Pam asking about 'Laverne's Pies Tires Fixed Also' and the specific pie order negotiation
jokes.setup: Jim mentions a pie stand
jokes.punchline: The absurd business name and detailed pie preferences
jokes.explanation: Great absurd business name and believable couple dynamic about pie preferences.

jokes.text: Work Bus promotional video's corporate speak: 'In this age of belt-tightening and less empowered workers, a work bus is how tomorrow gets things done'
jokes.setup: Professional promotional video
jokes.punchline: Dystopian corporate language presented as positive
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of corporate promotional language. The dystopian undertones are well-crafted.

jokes.text: Kevin getting stuck and the 'Lose weight!' 'I'm trying, sorry' exchange
jokes.setup: Everyone's cramped on the work bus
jokes.punchline: Kevin literally getting stuck and the blunt response
jokes.explanation: Straightforward physical comedy. Works for character but relies on obvious physical humor.

jokes.text: Toby: 'I wish! No, I made some short lists, I had a couple of sleepovers, but I never managed to get in the end zone.'
jokes.setup: Andy assumes Toby was adopted
jokes.punchline: Toby's sad football metaphor about failed adoptions
jokes.explanation: Exceptionally dark and sad humor perfectly delivered by Toby. The football metaphor for failed childhood adoption is brilliant and heartbreaking.

jokes.text: Andy's medical circulation excuse met with 'Boy, I will hammerspank your rear'
jokes.setup: Andy keeps getting up claiming circulation issues
jokes.punchline: Phyllis's unexpected aggressive threat
jokes.explanation: Phyllis's unexpected aggression and the ridiculous word 'hammerspank' make this highly quotable and surprising.

jokes.text: Pam getting covered in coffee and the awkward aftermath
jokes.setup: Cramped bus conditions
jokes.punchline: Pam gets soaked and everyone's awkward reaction
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy setup. Functional but not particularly creative or memorable.

jokes.text: Jim's talking head: 'Really? Smirking? What can I say? I love justice.'
jokes.setup: Jim's prank has backfired spectacularly
jokes.punchline: His complete lack of remorse and twisted logic
jokes.explanation: Good Jim character moment - his ability to rationalize his pranks even when they backfire.

jokes.text: Andy's response to pie demands: 'All right! The fat people have spoken!'
jokes.setup: Office chanting for pies
jokes.punchline: Andy's inappropriate characterization of his employees
jokes.explanation: Inappropriate Andy humor - offensive but in character. The bluntness makes it quotable.

jokes.text: Bus driving away while someone is still outside with 'Stop. Come back. Too late.'
jokes.setup: Someone gets off the bus briefly
jokes.punchline: The matter-of-fact acceptance of being left behind
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery and acceptance of the situation. The economy of the punchline works well.

jokes.text: Pam's shabooya roll call: 'My name is Pam / I like to paint / You think you're better? / Oh, no, you ain't!'
jokes.setup: Innocent shabooya roll call game
jokes.punchline: Pam's unexpected aggressive turn
jokes.explanation: Great subversion - Pam starts sweet but turns competitive. Good character moment showing her hidden edge.

jokes.text: Kevin's shabooya: 'My name is Kevin / That is my name / They call me Kevin / 'Cause that's my name'
jokes.setup: Kevin's turn at the creative roll call
jokes.punchline: His complete lack of creativity or logic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin character moment - his literal-minded approach creates accidental absurdist poetry.

jokes.text: Kevin suddenly doing complex pie math in his head vs struggling with simple salad math
jokes.setup: Kevin can calculate pie math instantly
jokes.punchline: But fails completely with salad math
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character comedy - Kevin's selective competence based on his interests is perfectly executed. The contrast is beautifully crafted.

jokes.text: Dwight's breakdown: 'You win, Jim! You win... You are the alpha male... go buy the whole world a pie!' 'That's impossible.'
jokes.setup: Dwight finally gives in to Jim's demands
jokes.punchline: His dramatic capitulation followed by Jim's literal response
jokes.explanation: Great escalation and character moment. Dwight's dramatic surrender contrasted with Jim's literal response works well.

jokes.text: Dwight abandoning them at the gas station
jokes.setup: Dwight's having a breakdown
jokes.punchline: He literally drives away from his own employees
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Dwight's breakdown. The abandonment is shocking and in character.

jokes.text: Dwight's fertility confession: 'I'm barren, Jim. My trouser hives are void of honey.'
jokes.setup: Jim asks why Dwight's being a jerk
jokes.punchline: Dwight's poetic euphemism for infertility
jokes.explanation: Exceptional character moment - Dwight's poetic language for devastating news is perfectly him. 'Trouser hives void of honey' is brilliant and quotable.

jokes.text: Dwight: 'I thought I would be a father and instead I am a eunuch, neutered by my own building.'
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining his fertility issues
jokes.punchline: His dramatic self-diagnosis about his building
jokes.explanation: Excellent continuation of Dwight's poetic despair. The dramatic language and self-blame are perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Jim's prank reveal: 'Dwight, that was a prank.' Dwight: 'That's genius. That's the best prank you've ever done.'
jokes.setup: Dwight thinks EMF radiation made him infertile
jokes.punchline: He's impressed by the imaginary prank's thoroughness
jokes.explanation: Great ironic moment - Dwight admires a prank that didn't actually happen. Good character consistency in his respect for elaborate pranks.

jokes.text: Andy on Toby's letter: 'It's inaccurate, dishonest, and in a word, dong water.'
jokes.setup: Andy reviews Toby's adoption reference letter
jokes.punchline: His juvenile and nonsensical insult
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy juvenile humor. 'Dong water' is memorable but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Jim's inappropriate fertility questions to Dwight about positions and conception time
jokes.setup: Jim tries to console Dwight about fertility
jokes.punchline: His increasingly invasive personal questions
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy but relies somewhat on shock value. The escalation of inappropriate questions works.

jokes.text: Dwight seeing Nellie crying and Jim's confused 'I don't think that's Nellie'
jokes.setup: Dwight makes a comment about British women being emotional
jokes.punchline: They're looking at a completely different crying woman
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection gag. The mistaken identity creates a nice absurd moment.

jokes.text: Dwight's 'bildenkinder' explanation - childless landlords calling tenants their children
jokes.setup: Jim suggests Dwight's tenants are like his children
jokes.punchline: Dwight's specific German term for this pathetic behavior
jokes.explanation: Excellent Dwight character moment - his specific knowledge and self-aware explanation is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Andy's reference letter changes: 'She'll make a wonderful mother to any child who can overlook weird accents'
jokes.setup: Andy agrees to sign Toby's letter with changes
jokes.punchline: His backhanded compliment about Toby's accent
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Andy's inability to be completely kind even when trying to help.

jokes.text: Kevin trying to provoke Oscar into a pie fight: 'You don't have the guts, you stupid, dumb do-do face'
jokes.setup: Kevin wants to get hit with pie
jokes.punchline: His juvenile insults trying to provoke Oscar
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - Kevin's transparent manipulation and childish insults are in character.

jokes.text: Andy's shabooya: 'My name is Andy / I don't do drugs / Now check the style / Of Flatt and Scruggs!'
jokes.setup: Andy's turn at the roll call
jokes.punchline: His random bluegrass music reference
jokes.explanation: Good Andy character moment - his unexpected musical knowledge and attempt to be cool. The Flatt and Scruggs reference is perfectly random for him.

tmdb_overview: The staffers hit the road in a rented bus when the building is deemed unsafe. Meanwhile, Nellie reaches out to Andy for help in adopting a baby, and Jim uses pie to spread cheer to Pam.
